content: ADR management: (1) Assess severity and temporal relationship to drug exposure; (2) For Type A reactions, reduce dose or discontinue drug; (3) For Type B reactions, discontinue immediately and avoid rechallenge; (4) Provide supportive care (antihistamines for allergic reactions, corticosteroids for severe hypersensitivity, epinephrine for anaphylaxis); (5) Report serious ADRs to FDA MedWatch or national pharmacovigilance systems; (6) Document in patient record as allergy/intolerance; (7) Consider alternative therapies. Rechallenge is contraindicated for severe Type B reactions. Desensitization protocols exist for certain essential drugs (penicillin, chemotherapy). Prevention includes therapeutic drug monitoring for narrow therapeutic index drugs and pharmacogenomic testing where indicated.
